question: Define a C++ `struct` named `Book` with members `title` (std::string), `author` (std::string), and `yearPublished` (int). Write a function `printBookDetails` that takes a `Book` struct (by value) and prints all its details. Demonstrate by creating a `Book` instance and calling the function.

test_cases.expected:
--- Book 1 ---
Book Details:
Title: The Great C++ Book
Author: N/A
Year Published: 2023

--- Book 2 ---
Book Details:
Title: N/A
Author: Jane Doe
Year Published: 0

--- Book 3 ---
Book Details:
Title: Another Book
Author: John Smith
Year Published: -100

--- Book 4 ---
Book Details:
Title: Valid Title
Author: Valid Author
Year Published: 1999

errors: The `printBookDetails` function demonstrates inconsistent robustness in handling 'missing' or 'invalid' data. While it explicitly checks for and handles empty strings for `title` and `author` by printing 'N/A', it directly prints `yearPublished` even if it's 0 or negative. In many real-world applications, 0 or negative years would signify an unknown or invalid publication date, and a more robust implementation would apply consistent validation or provide a more informative representation (e.g., 'Unknown Year') for such cases, similar to how empty strings are handled.
